sprint: Sprint 29 -- V0.27.3 Bug Fixes (drive_summary 12-field + DriveDetector + calibration + drive_counter)
backlogItem: Bug-fix sprint #2 in the V0.27 chain. Per CIO 2026-05-10 chain-end-merge rule: V0.27.3 branches FROM sprint/sprint28-bugfixes-V0.27.2 (cumulative chain), NOT from main. Main waits for the WHOLE V0.27 chain to be 'fully functional working' before any merge. V0.27.3 closes 5 bugs surfaced during V0.27.2 deploy + Spool's 2026-05-10 evening test cycle.

testBaseline.note: Post-V0.27.2 baseline (V0.27.2 sprint-tip @ 61ec3c7). Sprint 28 added 4149 fast tests per Rex Session 188 close. Verify exact count at sprint start.

sprintNotes: GOAL: Close the 4 bugs queued for V0.27.3 per CIO 2026-05-10 finalized stack. P1 stack (3): drive_summary 12-field writer (B-059 / Spool Spec 3) + DriveDetector warm-restart misses short trips (I-019) + calibration.py types.py shadow + missing baselines table (I-018). P3 (1): drive_counter server-side sync gap (B-064). US-313 (B-062 drain_event close) DROPPED 2026-05-10 per CIO -- empirical Drain Test 11 + historical DB review show drains 10/11/12/13/14 all closed cleanly; Spool's 4-of-4 unclosed report did not match DB state. B-062 closes wontfix; US-307 forensic instrumentation already shipped in V0.27.2 stands-watch for any future occurrence.

EXECUTION ORDER (deps-aware): TIER 1 (P1, parallel) -- US-310 (drive_summary 12-field) + US-311 (DriveDetector warm-restart) + US-312 (calibration.py rename + baselines migration). TIER 2 (P3, parallel-with-Tier-1) -- US-314 (drive_counter sync gap).

THEME: 'V0.27.2 deploy + bench Drain 11 unmasked the next layer.' Bugs surfaced from Sprint 28 IRL operational test (Drives 8/9/10) + Spool's housekeeping clean-up of obd2db + bench drain forensics. Each story has a synthetic test that would FAIL pre-fix (per feedback_runtime_validation_required.md) AND a real-world validation gate captured in the sprint validation block.

CONSTRAINTS (anti-blocker discipline + Sprint 28 lessons): (a) US-274 file-existence check ran during grooming. (b) US-282 commit-vs-claim verifier active. (c) BL-010 + verify-table-names rule applied. (d) feedback_pm_verify_diagnostic_premises.md: PM reproduced calibration.py shadow empirically + REFUTED Spool's DriveDetector debounce hypothesis (MIN_INTER_DRIVE_SECONDS=5 not minutes); story scopes reflect verified premises. (e) feedback_pm_run_pre_flight_during_grooming.md: PM ran each story's pre-flight rg during grooming + baked findings into scope.filesToTouch (Sprint 28 had 50% pre-flight defect rate; this sprint targets 0). (f) CIO 2026-05-10 chain-end-merge: ALL implementation commits land on sprint/sprint29-bugfixes-V0.27.3; nothing merges to main until WHOLE V0.27 chain is fully functional working system.

GROUNDING SOURCES: Spool 2026-05-09 sprint-28-bug-fixes-consolidated note (Bug 2 P1 reclassification = B-059 Spec 3). Spool 2026-05-10 three-drives-tonight note (Bug A DriveDetector = I-019; Bug B drain close = B-062; drive_counter sync gap = B-064). CIO 2026-05-09 directive (calibration.py = I-018). Plus V0.27.2 BL-012 + BL-013 context (forensic instrumentation evidence + recorder seam pattern).

POST-SPRINT-29 ACTION ITEMS (NOT in sprint.json per CIO no-human-tasks rule): (1) CIO completes B-063 fuse-box wiring (still gated for in-vehicle drives). (2) Drive 11+ post-fuse-box first clean car-coupled drive. (3) Drain Test 12 post-V0.27.3 deploy validates B-062 fix held. (4) Once V0.27 chain fully clean, /chain-validated (or equivalent) merges all V0.27.X to main as new fully-functional-working stable.

stories.title: drive_summary 12-field writer contract enforcement (B-059)
stories.intent: Spool Spec 3 + housekeeping Item 2 P1: drives 3+4+5 currently have NULL on all analytics columns (start_time / end_time / duration_seconds / row_count / is_real / data_source). Pi-sync wrote keys; server-side analytics writer that populates fields 3-8 never ran. Drives 6-10 have NO rows because the broader US-304 writer regression blocked them entirely (now fixed in V0.27.2). V0.27.3 fixes the SERVER-SIDE analytics writer per Spool's 12-field contract: every drive_end + sync round-trip should produce a fully-populated drive_summary row within 30s. Without this, Spool's per-drive grading + analysis_history + drive_statistics are downstream-blocked.

filesToTouch: src/server/services/analysis.py (UPDATE -- _ensureDriveSummary at line 758 populates fields 3-8 per Spool 12-field contract: device_id + drive_id + start_time + end_time + duration_seconds + row_count + is_real + data_source + drive_start_timestamp + ambient_temp_at_start_c + starting_battery_v + barometric_kpa_at_start. Respect _PRESERVE_ON_UPDATE; race-handling per Spec 3 -- Pi-sync writes 1+2+9-12 first, analytics writes 3-8 second; UPSERT by (source_device, source_id) with NULL preservation)

tests/server/test_drive_summary_12field_contract.py (NEW -- integration test exercises Pi sync -> analytics writer -> 12-field row; asserts fields populated within 30s of drive_end; would FAIL pre-fix because analytics path doesn't fire OR fields stay NULL)

scripts/backfill_drive_summary_analytics_fields.py (NEW -- one-shot backfill for drives 3-10; reads realtime_data per drive_id + writes start_time/end_time/duration_seconds/row_count/is_real/data_source; idempotent + --dry-run)
filesToRead: offices/pm/backlog/B-059-drive-summary-writer-contract-12-fields.md (full Spec 3 contract + race-handling rules + insufficient-data semantics)
src/server/services/analysis.py existing _ensureDriveSummary (line 758) + caller path at line 927
doNotTouch: Pi-side drive_summary writer (US-304 fixed in V0.27.2; out of scope here)
Pi-sync upsert semantics (sync.py contract; consumed not modified)

groundingRefs.value: Spool housekeeping Item 2 evidence: drives 3+4+5 have rows where every analytics column is NULL; drives 6-10 have no rows at all (US-304 broader regression masked this layer)
Spec 3 12-field contract is consumer-defined; race semantics + insufficient-data branch (row_count<100 -> is_real=NULL; start_time IS NULL -> is_real=FALSE stub row)
Real-world validation gate: Drive 11+ post-fuse-box-wiring -> sync round-trip -> server drive_summary row has all 12 fields populated; backfill cleans drives 3-10

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: rg `_ensureDriveSummary|drive_summary.*INSERT|drive_summary.*UPDATE` src/server/services/analysis.py + read context at line 758 + caller at line 927; document trigger path in completionNotes
_ensureDriveSummary populates all 12 fields per Spec 3 contract; analytics-side fields (3-8) write within 30s of drive_end + sync arrival
tests/server/test_drive_summary_12field_contract.py: full Pi-capture -> sync -> analytics writer flow asserts 12-field population; would FAIL pre-fix
invariants: Synthetic test FAILS against pre-fix code (analytics fields not populated)
Real-world validation gate: post-fuse-box first clean drive (Drive 11+) MUST produce drive_summary row with all 12 fields populated within 30s of sync completion
Backfill script does NOT modify rows that already have populated analytics columns (idempotent)
stopConditions: If pre-flight audit reveals _ensureDriveSummary is structurally absent OR not on the drive-end trigger path (e.g., dead code) -- STOP, document; PM re-scopes
If backfill script reveals a drive's realtime_data range cannot be determined (e.g., I-019 orphan rows) -- STOP, defer that drive's backfill until US-311 ships

stories.title: DriveDetector warm-restart misses short trips (I-019)
stories.intent: I-019 Bug A: Spool 2026-05-10 evening test captured 1,078 NULL-drive_id rows in a 37-min orphan window between Drive 8 and Drive 9. A 2-3 min around-the-block trip in that window never fired drive_start / drive_end. Spool's 'debounce window' hypothesis was REFUTED by PM pre-flight (MIN_INTER_DRIVE_SECONDS=5; Mike waited 1+ min between drives; debounce wouldn't fire). Bug exists but root cause is elsewhere -- four candidate paths to investigate (PowerDownOrchestrator KEY_OFF state-reset / connection_log state tracking / RPM-poll pause-resume / ApplicationOrchestrator init blocker on warm-restart). V0.27.3 ships investigation + targeted fix + integration test.

filesToTouch: src/pi/obdii/drive/detector.py (UPDATE -- targeted fix in DriveDetector state machine; specific change depends on Ralph pre-flight identifying root cause among the 4 candidate paths)
src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/lifecycle.py (UPDATE only if root cause is in warm-restart init path; otherwise out of scope -- document drift in completionNotes)

tests/pi/integration/test_drive_detector_warm_restart_e2e.py (NEW -- synthetic Test A/B/C protocol from I-019 spec: cold drive A -> 2-5 min wait -> short drive B -> 5-10 min wait -> cold drive C; asserts drive_id=N/N+1/N+2 all assigned; would FAIL pre-fix because Test B is missed)

scripts/backfill_orphan_drive_id.py (NEW -- one-shot backfill: assigns drive_id to the 1,078 NULL-tagged rows from 2026-05-09T23:40 -> 2026-05-10T00:16Z based on RPM > 500 windows; --dry-run preview + idempotent)
filesToRead: offices/pm/issues/I-019-drivedetector-warm-restart-misses-short-trips.md (full bug spec + 4 hypotheses + Test A/B/C repro protocol)
src/pi/obdii/drive/detector.py FULL state machine (DriveState enum + _checkDriveStart + _checkDriveEnd + transitions)
doNotTouch: MIN_INTER_DRIVE_SECONDS = 5 (debounce hypothesis already refuted; constant correct)

groundingRefs.value: Empirical evidence (2026-05-10): Drive 8 clean (8268 rows), 37-min orphan window with 1078 NULL rows + ~3 min real driving in middle, Drive 9 captured. DriveDetector NEVER fired drive_start for the around-the-block test
Hypothesis-refuted-at-pre-flight: Spool said debounce window. Code shows MIN_INTER_DRIVE_SECONDS=5; 1+ min gap wouldn't trip. Real cause is elsewhere -- 4 candidate paths in I-019
Real-world validation gate: post-fix Test A/B/C protocol on Drive 12+ (post-fuse-box) MUST produce drive_id=N/N+1/N+2 (no orphan); requires B-063 hardware fix

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: read full DriveDetector state machine (detector.py); investigate the 4 candidate hypothesis paths; document which root cause matches in completionNotes
Targeted fix lands per identified root cause (single-file or multi-file as needed; phantom-path drift documented per BL-010 pattern if scope shifts)
tests/pi/integration/test_drive_detector_warm_restart_e2e.py: Test A/B/C protocol asserts drive_id=N/N+1/N+2 all fire; would FAIL pre-fix (Test B missed)
scripts/backfill_orphan_drive_id.py populates the 1,078 NULL-tagged rows with the synthetic drive_id; idempotent + --dry-run
invariants: Synthetic Test A/B/C FAILS pre-fix; passes post-fix
Real-world validation gate: B-063 fuse-box wiring complete BEFORE running IRL Test A/B/C drill (running on flaky USB-C power confounds with brownout-throttling)
Existing DriveDetector tests stay GREEN (no regression in cold-start / long-drive paths)
stopConditions: If pre-flight audit reveals the bug requires new schema (e.g., flag column on drive_summary or new state-tracking table) -- STOP, file as architectural-ambiguity blocker
If 4-hypothesis investigation surfaces NONE of the candidate paths and bug is something else entirely -- STOP, document new hypothesis; PM revises scope

stories.title: calibration.py types.py shadow + missing baselines table (I-018)
stories.intent: I-018: `python src/server/analytics/calibration.py --calibrate --apply` crashes at import time due to TWO stacked failures. (1) `src/server/analytics/types.py` shadows the Python stdlib `types` module -- when calibration.py imports statistics -> re -> enum -> stdlib types, Python finds the local types.py instead. Same anti-pattern class as I-014 (Sprint 12 obd -> obdii rename). (2) Even if fix #1 lands, the `baselines` table doesn't exist on chi-srv-01 -- `src/server/db/models.py:703-728` defines the Baseline model but no migration creates it. V0.27.3 ships both fixes coupled per CIO 2026-05-10 'keep it lean' directive: rename + 4 importer updates + v0008 migration + integration test that the calibration CLI runs to completion.

filesToTouch: src/server/analytics/types.py (RENAME -- to src/server/analytics/analytics_types.py; preserves all current contents. Pre-flight rg surfaces the FULL importer list -- advanced.py + basic.py + helpers.py + calibration.py + __init__.py -- pre-flight audit step bakes those into commit; phantom-path-drift documented per BL-010 if any importer found outside the scope.filesToRead list)

src/server/analytics/__init__.py (UPDATE -- re-export path updates from `from src.server.analytics.types` -> `from src.server.analytics.analytics_types`; downstream consumers stay unchanged via package-level re-export)

src/server/analytics/calibration.py (UPDATE -- the original crashing file; remove any local import of analytics types if applicable; ensure `import statistics` resolves to stdlib correctly post-rename)
src/server/migrations/versions/v0008_us312_create_baselines.py (NEW -- creates baselines table per src/server/db/models.py:703-728 Baseline model schema; idempotent CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS)

tests/server/test_calibration_cli_integration.py (NEW -- integration test invokes calibration.py CLI against test DB; asserts exit-0 + baselines rows written; would FAIL pre-fix on import error or missing-table error)
filesToRead: src/server/analytics/calibration.py (entry that crashes at line 52 import)
doNotTouch: src/server/ai/types.py (DIFFERENT file, not the shadowing source; out of scope)
Baseline model class schema (frozen; migration just creates table per model)
calibration.py business logic (only the import path needs to work)

groundingRefs.value: Empirical reproduction (PM 2026-05-09): calibration.py CLI ImportError 'cannot import name GenericAlias from types' -- Python's own error names the fix
5 importers identified at PM pre-flight: src/server/analytics/{advanced,basic,helpers,__init__}.py + calibration.py itself; rename + update is mechanical
Baseline model exists at src/server/db/models.py:703-728 but no migration creates the table -- Spool housekeeping Item 1 evidence: SHOW TABLES on obd2db does not include baselines
Same anti-pattern as I-014 (Sprint 12 obd -> obdii rename); standing rule: NEVER name a local module the same as a Python stdlib module

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: rg `from src.server.analytics.types|from .types` src/ tests/ -- enumerate all importers; bake findings into commit + verify no missed callsites
src/server/analytics/types.py renamed to analytics_types.py; all importers updated; full fast suite GREEN post-rename
v0008 migration creates baselines table; apply_server_migrations runs cleanly + idempotently; SHOW TABLES on obd2db includes baselines post-deploy

tests/server/test_calibration_cli_integration.py: invokes calibration.py CLI end-to-end; asserts exit-0 + at least one baselines row written; would FAIL pre-fix on either import-error or missing-table error
verification: pytest tests/ -m 'not slow' -q (full fast suite GREEN; no broken imports)
invariants: Synthetic test FAILS pre-fix on import-error layer; FAILS post-rename pre-migration on missing-table layer; passes post-fix
Real-world validation gate: post-deploy on chi-srv-01, calibration CLI runs cleanly + writes rows to baselines table for drives 5+6+7
All existing analytics tests stay GREEN post-rename (no broken imports)
stopConditions: If rename + importer updates surface a circular-import or other import-graph problem -- STOP, audit; propose minimum-rework
If v0008 migration conflicts with an existing partial-state baselines table on chi-srv-01 -- STOP, document; PM approves cleanup before migration ships

stories.title: drive_counter server-side sync gap (B-064)
stories.intent: B-064: Pi has minted drive_id up to 10; server `obd2db.drive_counter.last_drive_id=3` (last good sync was Drive 3 era). Mirror writer for drive_counter table either isn't running or isn't covered by sync. Likely same Sweep-5 wiring drift family as US-304 + US-306. Not safety-critical -- realtime_data + connection_log + drive_summary all sync correctly -- but stale signal anyone querying drive_counter would trip over.

filesToTouch: src/pi/data/sync_log.py (UPDATE -- Pi-side cursor table writer; ensure drive_counter is in sync coverage list + delta-push covered. Pre-flight identifies whether actual fix lives here OR in src/pi/sync/client.py; phantom-path drift documented per BL-010 if scope shifts)

tests/pi/sync/test_drive_counter_sync.py (NEW -- integration test asserts Pi-side drive_counter advance triggers server-side update within 60s of next sync; would FAIL pre-fix because table is not in sync coverage)
filesToRead: src/pi/obdii/drive_id.py + drive_summary.py (Pi mints drive_id + writes drive_counter)
doNotTouch: drive_id minting logic on Pi (working correctly)
Server-side endpoints other than sync handler

groundingRefs.value: Empirical evidence (Spool 2026-05-10): SELECT last_drive_id FROM drive_counter on chi-srv-01 returns 3; Pi has minted up to 10 (next = 11). Last good sync was Drive 3 era (2026-04-23)
Likely same Sweep-5 wiring drift family as US-304 + US-306 (drive_id propagation lost in mixin refactor)
Real-world validation gate: post-V0.27.3 deploy + next IRL drive sync round-trip -> server drive_counter.last_drive_id matches Pi's most recent drive within 60s

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: rg `drive_counter` src/pi/sync/ + src/server/api/ -- map current sync coverage; identify why mirror isn't firing
drive_counter table covered by Pi sync delta push; server-side update propagates within 60s of sync completion
tests/pi/sync/test_drive_counter_sync.py: synthetic Pi mints drive_id=N -> sync triggers -> server drive_counter shows N within 60s; would FAIL pre-fix
invariants: Real-world validation gate: next IRL drive sync round-trip advances server drive_counter.last_drive_id to current Pi value
Existing realtime_data + connection_log + drive_summary sync paths unchanged
stopConditions: If sync coverage list location is NOT in src/pi/sync/ as expected -- STOP, document phantom-path drift (BL-010 pattern); ship to actual location
If the fix requires server-side schema change OR new sync endpoint -- STOP, file as architectural-ambiguity blocker
